scope_note: QA Fibonacci Resonance Cert family [219]: certifies that mean-motion resonances in the solar system AND exoplanet systems preferentially select Fibonacci ratios (p:q where both p,q are Fibonacci numbers) over non-Fibonacci ratios of the same resonance order. 60 resonances across 8 independent planetary systems. Order-1 result: 33/43 (77%) are Fibonacci (2:1 or 3:2) vs 22% expected under uniform selection from 9 coprime order-1 ratios. Binomial p=4.71e-14 (order-1), p=4.19e-12 (all-instances, 45/60 vs 31.25% expected). Unique-ratio binomial p=0.040. QA interpretation: T-operator = Fibonacci shift [[0,1],[1,1]] makes Fibonacci-ratio period locks dynamically deeper attractors. Tier 2→3 candidate. VERIFIED (2026-07-04 audit, cross-checked against Wall, D.D. (1960) DOI:10.2307/2309169 integer-sequence conventions): independently recomputed TRAPPIST-1's actual published orbital periods and confirmed all 6 consecutive-pair resonance ratios match the catalogue exactly; recomputed the headline binomial p-values via exact math.comb, matching the published paper's stated 4.2e-12/4.7e-14. Found the fixture's declared p_value/order1_p_value were stale '1e-06' placeholders off by 6+ orders of magnitude -- fixed, and added FR_RECOMPUTE (stdlib exact binomial recomputation cross-checked against the catalogue). Corrective renumber: [163] is reserved by QA Dead Reckoning; Fibonacci Resonance uses [219]. Sources: Murray & Dermott (1999), Luger et al. (2017), Luque et al. (2024), NASA Exoplanet Archive.
